cancer_history_summary:
Age: 19  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Osteosarcoma (high‑grade conventional) of the distal right femur  
Histology: High‑grade conventional osteosarcoma  
Current extent: Metastatic – solitary pulmonary nodule in the right lower lobe (oligometastatic)  

Biomarkers: No molecular/genetic results reported; comprehensive sarcoma‑focused NGS ordered but pending.  

Treatment history:  
# Approx 2024: Limb‑sparing surgical resection of distal right femur with endoprosthetic reconstruction (curative intent)  
# Approx 2024‑2025: Adjuvant MAP chemotherapy (methotrexate, doxorubicin, cisplatin) – six cycles completed, no major complications  
# Early 2025 (exact dates not recorded): Two cycles of ifosfamide/etoposide (administered following suspicion of metastatic disease)  
# 6/25/2025‑present: Surveillance chest CT identifies solitary 2.0 × 1.8 cm right lower‑lobe nodule consistent with metastatic disease; awaiting tissue confirmation. Planned options include video‑assisted thoracoscopic wedge resection or stereotactic body radiotherapy (SBRT).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, liver dysfunction, HIV, or hepatitis infections.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68  
Sex: Male  

Cancer type: Cutaneous malignant melanoma (left upper extremity)  
Histology: Malignant melanoma, cutaneous origin  
Current extent: Metastatic (bilateral pulmonary metastases), currently partial response to therapy  

Biomarkers: BRAFV600E mutation (confirmed), NRAS wild‑type, PD‑L1 status not reported  

Treatment history:  
# Surgery (wide local excision + sentinel lymph node dissection) – performed after diagnosis at age 65 (circa 2022); pathology showed 2/3 nodes positive with extracapsular extension (stage IIIC).  
# 6/2024 – 5/2025: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 weeks (adjuvant). Completed 12 months; disease‐free on imaging until progression noted on 6/22/2025 PET‑CT. Best response: Complete remission of detectable disease during therapy.  
# 6/2025 – ~8/2025: Combined ipilimumab 3 mg/kg IV + nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 21 days (planned 4 induction cycles). Cycles 1‑2 tolerated with Grade 1 pruritus; after Cycle 2 imaging (6 weeks) showed ≈33 % reduction in pulmonary nodules → Partial Response per RECIST v1.1.  
# ~8/2025: After third ipilimumab infusion, developed Grade 3 immune‑mediated colitis requiring hospitalisation; ipilimumab discontinued permanently.  
# 9/2025 present: Nivolumab maintenance 240 mg IV q2 weeks (monotherapy). Ongoing; disease remains in partial response; labs stable, no new toxicities reported.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases.  
No lack of measurable disease (pulmonary nodules measurable).  
No documentation of congestive heart failure.  
No pneumonitis observed on imaging or clinically.  
Renal function normal (creatinine 0.92 mg/dL).  
Liver function normal (AST 31 U/L, ALT 34 U/L, bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL).  
No recorded HIV or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 55  
Sex: Female  

Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: Low‑grade serous carcinoma (LGSC)  

Current extent: Recurrent/advanced intra‑abdominal disease with multiple subcentimeter peritoneal implants, FDG‑avid pelvic sidewall lesions (most recent SUV max ~7.8) and a 2 cm para‑aortic lymph node; otherwise no distant organ or bony metastases evident on imaging as of 24 June 2025.  

Biomarkers: KRAS p.G12D dominant clone (variant allele frequency ≈45 %), co‑existent NRAS Q61K subclone (low VAF), CCNE1 copy‑number gain (~3‑fold). Serial CA‑125 elevations noted (85 U/mL → 118 U/mL → 210 U/mL in June 2025; subsequent value 62 U/mL on repeat labs).  

Treatment history:  
# Approximately 2023 (age 53): Primary cytoreductive surgery – total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo‑oophorectomy, comprehensive surgical staging.  
# 11/2023 – 02/2024: Four cycles adjuvant carboplatin (AUC 5) plus paclitaxel (175 mg/m²) – completed 14 months prior to 22 Jun 2025; tolerated with grade 2 nausea (controlled).  
# 03/2025 present: Letrozole 2.5 mg orally once daily – initiated 3 months prior to 22 Jun 2025; continued uninterrupted. Disease assessment indicates stabilization of peritoneal implants on CT (28 Jun 2025) after earlier PET‑CT showed new FDG‑avid pelvic sidewall foci suggesting progression.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (Cr 0.85 mg/dL on 23 Jun 2025 labs), hepatic dysfunction (AST 21 U/L, ALT 19 U/L), HIV infection, or chronic viral hepatitis.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 45  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma (mediastinal primary)  
Histology: Precursor T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma, TdT⁺, CD3⁺, CD7⁺, CD20⁻, CD19⁻, CD22⁻  
Current extent: Metastatic/progressive disease with mediastinal residual mass, bilateral pulmonary nodules, multiple hepatic lesions, probable splenic and bone‑marrow involvement (Stage II originally, now systemic)  
Biomarkers: NOTCH1 V1578F mutation; PTEN loss; JAK3 V678M mutation; TAL1‑ST5 fusion; CD20‑negative, CD19‑negative, CD22‑negative  

Treatment history:  
# Hyper‑CVAD ± high‑dose MTX/cytarabine – 4 cycles (completed prior to 2025‑04, resulted in partial response)  
# Nelarabine – 2 cycles (salvage, completed prior to 2025‑05, achieved stable disease)  
# Therapeutic anticoagulation for right popliteal/femoral DVT: enoxaparin 1 mg/kg SC q12h beginning ≈2025‑05‑11, transitioned to apixaban 5 mg PO BID (ongoing as of 2025‑06‑22)  
# Palliative mediastinal external beam radiation therapy – 30 Gy in 10 fractions, delivered roughly 2025‑05‑30 to 2025‑06‑12 (completion confirmed on 2025‑06‑23 CT)  
# Prednisone 40 mg PO daily ×5 days with taper (initiated 2025‑06‑22 for hepatic inflammation)  
# Planned supportive dosing modifications (documented 2025‑06‑22): bortezomib 1.0 mg/m² SC on Days 1, 8, 15 of each 28‑day cycle; discontinuation of vincristine after Cycle 1 if neuropathy worsens (implementation pending)  
# Investigational therapy discussions (as of 2025‑06‑25): potential enrolment in early‑phase trials targeting NOTCH1 (γ‑secretase inhibitor), JAK inhibition (ruxolitinib), PI3Kδ inhibition, CD3‑bispecific antibodies, CAR‑T directed at TRBV families, or off‑label pembrolizumab (not yet commenced)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
Evidence of hepatic dysfunction – grade 3 transaminitis (AST/ALT >5 × upper limit of normal) recorded on 2025‑06‑22 associated with hepatic metastatic involvement.  
No documented uncontrolled brain metastases.  
No evidence of congestive heart failure.  
No evidence of pneumonitis.  
Renal function reported as stable; no indication of renal impairment.  
No documented HIV or chronic hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer  
Histology: High‑grade serous carcinoma  
Current extent: FIGO Stage IV (metastatic), presently stable/metastatically controlled  
Biomarkers: CA‑125 48 U/mL (stable vs prior 44 U/mL)  

Treatment history:  
# 2024‑??‑?? to present: Rucaparib 600 mg PO twice daily (ongoing; tolerating grade 2 anemia/fatigue)  
# Approx. Mar 2025 (≈3 months prior to 6/20/2025): Percutaneous radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis (single procedure) – post‑procedure imaging shows expected necrosis, disease otherwise stable  
# Approx. Mar 2025 (≈3 months prior to 6/20/2025): Stereotactic body radiation therapy to solitary para‑aortic lymph node, 30 Gy in 5 fractions – subsequent imaging shows fibrosis, no residual activity  

Response: Serial CT scans (June 2025) demonstrate unchanged size of treated hepatic lesion (now 3.8 cm with central necrosis consistent with post‑treatment change) and stability of all other visceral metastases; no new lesions, ascites absent, para‑aortic node fibrotic. Overall assessment – stable metastatic disease.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction (creatinine 0.84 mg/dL), clinically meaningful liver dysfunction (AST 38 U/L, ALT 42 U/L, Alk Phos 112 U/L), HIV, or hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Prostate cancer  
Histology: Adenocarcinoma, Gleason score 9 (4 + 5)  
Current extent: Metastatic castration‑resistant (bone‑only disease, no visceral metastases confirmed by CT chest/abdomen/pelvis 2025‑06‑25)  
Biomarkers: Serial PSA – baseline postoperative nadir 0.18 ng/mL; rose to 0.9 ng/mL prompting systemic therapy; most recent rise from 0.4 → 2.6 ng/mL over 6 weeks indicating biochemical progression; testosterone consistently <20 ng/dL (castrate level). Genomic sequencing (DDR panel) ordered, results pending.  

Treatment history:  
# Date unspecified – Surgery: radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymph node dissection (curative intent).  
# Ongoing – Androgen deprivation therapy (leuprolide depot injections) started perioperatively, continues through all subsequent entries.  
# Approx 2024‑06‑01 to 2025‑06‑19: Abiraterone acetate 1000 mg PO daily + low‑dose prednisone 5 mg BID (started for rising PSA and osseous disease). Tolerated with intermittent hot flashes; developed grade 2 essential hypertension managed with amlodipine 5 mg daily. Discontinued abiraterone on 2025‑06‑19; prednisone tapered over 1 week.  
# 2025‑06‑19 present: Enzalutamide 160 mg PO daily initiated; continuation planned.  
# Chemotherapy (docetaxel 75 mg/m² IV q21d ± prednisone 5 mg BID) discussed but not started as of latest note.  
# Radium‑223 dichloride considered for symptomatic bone‑dominant disease; not administered yet.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction, significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or active hepatitis. Controlled grade 2 essential hypertension on amlodipine noted (BP average 138/84 mmHg on 2025‑06‑19).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 63  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Small‑cell lung carcinoma (extensive‑stage)  
Histology: Small‑cell carcinoma  
Current extent: Progressive disease – new solitary hepatic metastasis (segment VII, 1.6 cm, SUVmax 7.8) with otherwise stable extracranial sites; no intracranial disease.  
Biomarkers: Not reported  

Treatment history:  
# Early 2025 (approximately January–April 2025): Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etomidate (etoposide) 100 mg/m² Day 1‑3 q21d, total 4 cycles; concurrent palliative external beam radiation to L3 (single 8 Gy fraction). Achieved partial response ≈30 % tumor reduction on Cycle 2 CT; subsequent scans showed stable disease. Toxicities – Grade 3 neutropenia (treated with filgristim), Grade 3 thrombocytopenia requiring transfusion, Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy.  
# Mid‑2025 (initiated after completion of platinum/etoposide, approximately May 2025): Lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m² IV q21d. Completed ≥5 cycles. Resulted in modest decrease in left adrenal size, stable right femoral lytic lesion; developed Grade 2 anemia (Hb 9.0 g/dL) managed with oral iron, intermittent Grade 3 neutropenia leading to dose reduction after fifth cycle. Restaging PET‑CT performed 7 days prior to 2025‑06‑25 revealed new hepatic lesion meeting RECIST criteria for progressive disease.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
- No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases (brain MRI repeatedly negative up to 2025‑06‑25).  
- No documented congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, HIV, or hepatitis infections.  
- Mild hepatic dysfunction attributed to metastatic infiltration noted on imaging (new liver lesion, slight elevation of liver enzymes implied).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 62  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Glioblastoma, IDH‑wildtype (right parietal lobe)  
Histology: WHO Grade IV diffuse astrocytic tumour (glioblastoma)  
Current extent: Recurrent/progressive disease with new non‑enhancing infiltrative focus in right posterior parietal cortex; no distant extracranial metastases identified  

Biomarkers: IDH‑wildtype confirmed; additional molecular data not documented  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 06/2024–present: Surgical resection of right parietal glioblastoma (gross‑total resection) followed by adjuvant radiotherapy (details not specified)  
# 2024‑2025 (exact dates not documented): Temozolomide combined with investigational PDGFRα inhibitor (experimental protocol) – discontinued due to disease progression evident on 05/2025 MRI  
# Early 2025 (age 63 according to note, presumed Jan 2025): Initiated bevacizumab 10 mg/kg intravenously every 14 days – achieved transient symptomatic benefit  
# Mid‑2025 (dose held): Bevacizumab temporarily stopped owing to grade 3 proteinuria (renal adverse event) – date of interruption not precisely recorded, inferred between January and May 2025  
# 06/25/2025‑present: Resumed bevacizumab monotherapy 10 mg/kg q14 days (planned continuation) – current regimen as of last visit
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Evidence of renal dysfunction – grade 3 proteinuria attributed to bevacizumab therapy, resulting in temporary drug interruption (documented in 2025 neuro‑oncology progress note). No documentation of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant hepatic impairment, HIV, or hepatitis infections.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 66  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Breast cancer  
Histology: Invasive lobular carcinoma (right breast)  
Current extent: Metastatic (known liver lesion in segment IV and multiple osseous metastases in thoracic spine); disease classified as stable/responding to therapy  
Biomarkers: Hormone‑receptor positive (ER⁺/PR⁺), HER2‑low, PIK3CA E545K mutation, ESR1 D538G mutation  

Treatment history:  
# Approximately January 2023 (patient age 64) ‑ Present: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m² PO BID Days 1‑14 every 21 days; partial response per RECIST v1.1, disease stability confirmed on CT (June 2025). Dose holds applied for ≥Grade 2 hand‑foot syndrome, persistent diarrhea >Day 3, or ANC < 1500/µL.  
# Prior (dates not specifically recorded): Paclitaxel (taxane‑based chemotherapy); completed before initiation of capecitabine. Resulted in lingering Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, clinically significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or active hepatitis infection.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 34  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Appendiceal gastroenteropancreatic neuroendocrine tumour (well‑differentiated)  
Histology: Well‑differentiated NET, Grade 2 (Ki‑67 ≈ 8%)  
Current extent: Metastatic to liver (stable disease on imaging as of 6/25/2025)  
Biomarkers: Somatostatin receptor‑2 positive (⁶⁸Ga‑DOTATATE SUVmax ≈ 12); Ki‑67 ~8%  

Treatment history:  
# Approx. 2022–2024 (ages 25‑27): temozolomide + bevacizumab – partial radiographic response of peritoneal deposits.  
# Approximately 2024‑present: octreotide LAR 30 mg intramuscular every 28 days – continuous since post‑temozolomide; ongoing as of latest visit.  
# Approx. 2024 (age 31) – post‑PRRT baseline MRI indicates completion of Lu‑177‑DOTATATE peptide receptor radionuclide therapy; resulted in regression of hepatic lesion in segment III to ≤0.5 cm and durable control elsewhere.  
# 03/2025‑present: everolimus 10 mg orally daily added to octreotide LAR – disease stability demonstrated on contrast‑enhanced MRI (segments III and VI lesions unchanged).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, renal dysfunction, liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or hepatitis infection reported in the supplied records.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 64  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: Gastro‑esophageal junction adenocarcinoma  
Histology: Adenocarcinoma  
Current extent: Metastatic disease with stable hepatic metastases, progressive peritoneal carcinomatosis, new moderate‑volume ascites, slight enlargement of retroperitoneal lymph nodes; no new osseous lesions. Disease classified as progressive on systemic therapy.  
Biomarkers: HER2 positive (IHC 3+, ISH amplification); co‑amplifications of MET and FGFR2; KRAS p.G12D mutation; PIK3CA p.H1047R mutation; PD‑L1 combined positivity score (CPS) 15.  

Treatment history:  
# Unknown‑to‑present: CAPOX (capecitabine + oxaliplatin) plus trastuzumab – first‑line; resulted in disease stabilization followed by progression prompting regimen change.  
# Unknown‑to‑present: FOLFIRI (folinic acid, fluorouracil, irinotecan) – second‑line; led to further disease progression.  
# ≤2025‑06‑19: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) 200 mg IV every 3 weeks – third‑line monoclonal antibody therapy initiated after FOLFIRI; initial disease stability but radiographic progression demonstrated on 2025‑06‑20 CT. Ongoing at least until 2025‑06‑25 (last infusion recorded 6 days prior to progress note).  
# 2023‑approx.: Radiation therapy to solitary L3 vertebral metastasis (osteoblastic focus) – delivered locally; subsequent scans show sclerosis without new bony disease.  

Supportive/intervening care: Levothyroxine 75 µg daily for grade 2 hypothyroidism; intermittent ondansetron for nausea; ferrous sulfate 325 mg BID begun after transfusion for grade 3 anemia; plans for epoetin alfa pending iron repletion; therapeutic paracentesis contemplated; consideration of enrolment in HER2‑directed antibody‑drug conjugate trial (DS‑8201a/T‑DxD).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of uncontrolled brain metastases, lack of measurable disease, congestive heart failure, pneumonitis, significant renal dysfunction, clinically significant liver dysfunction, HIV infection, or active hepatitis infection documented in the supplied records.
